en: Please select all services that your company performs. It is common to select more than one.

Onsite Industrial or Construction Services
Services or operations performed at a client/operator site relating to the construction, demolition, maintenance or repair of equipment, buildings, or properties. Examples may include:
  • Construction
  • Installation
  • Maintenance
  • Electrical Work
  • Infrastructure
  • Equipment/Plant Operation
  • Demolition
  • Explosives
  • Operating/Maintaining Machinery
  • HVAC Repair/Installation
  • Working at Heights
  • Tower Climbing/Construction/Maintenance
  • Tree Trimming

Offsite Services
Services or operations not performed at a client/operator site or location. Examples may include:
  • Laboratories
  • Clinics
  • Warehouses & Storage
  • Fabrication Shops
  • Maintenance Facilities
  • Office & Administrative Buildings
  • Server Locations

Material Supplier or Manufacturing Services
Manufacturing, processing, supplying, or assembling raw materials or products. Examples may include:
  • Chemicals
  • Industrial Gases (not delivered via pipeline)
  • Electrical Components
  • Construction Materials
  • Electronics
  • Paper Products
  • Packaging
  • Consumables
  • Textiles
  • Apparel
  • Plastics & Rubber
  • Metals
  • Wholesale & Retail Trade
  • Fiberoptic cables
  • Furniture
  • Supplies (i.e. Office, Laboratory, Life Science Products, etc.)
  • Equipment Rental or Leasing without Operator

Transportation Services
Any party, person, agent, or carrier that provides freight, goods, or passenger transportation. Examples may include:
  • Air Transportation
  • Marine
  • Shuttle
  • Courier
  • Tanker
  • Delivery
  • Precious Cargo
  • Hauler
  • Trucking
  • Charter
  • Rail

Onsite Support Services
Activities performed at a client/operator site relating to facilities or property maintenance. Examples may include:
  • Exterminating & Pest Control
  • Locksmith
  • Janitorial
  • Landscaping/Lawn Care
  • Snow Removal
  • Emergency Services
  • Security
  • Food Services/Caterers
  • Vending Machines
  • Non-Hazardous Waste Collection (i.e. Garbage Collection)

IT, Hardware and Software Services
Providing services to facilitate the use of applications or technology. Examples may include:
  • Software Development and Engineering
  • Software (Systems & Application)
  • Internet Services
  • Hardware Installation and Servicing
  • Hardwire Networking
  • Server Installation and Maintenance
  • Cable
  • Satellite
  • TV and/or Communications
  • Wireless Data
  • Telephonic and Video Conference Services
  • Help Desk Services
  • IT, Engineering and Consulting Services

Professional, Technical, Engineering or Real Estate Services
Services provided using technical or industry expertise, typically requiring certifications or licenses. Examples may include:
  • Insurance Brokers
  • Legal Services
  • Accounting and Payroll Services
  • Financial Services
  • Design Services
  • Printing Services
  • Marketing
  • Advertising
  • Publishing
  • Surveying
  • Engineering & Consulting
  • Geophysical Surveying and Mapping
  • Architectural Services
  • Drafting
  • Building Inspection
  • Environmental Consulting
  • Real Estate
  • Rental and Leasing Services

Education Services
Services designed to engage, prepare, or teach others. Examples may include:
  • Technical and Trade Schools such as Apprenticeship Training programs
  • Professional and Management Development Training
  • Professional Schools
  • Health and Safety Training Services
  • First Aid and CPR (Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation) Instruction and Educational and Support Services
  • Exam Preparation Services
  • Professional Certification Services

Do your company's workers ever use or handle power, pneumatic, or hydraulic tools?\n\nExamples may include drills, circular saws, jackhammers, grinders, etc.
Do your company's workers ever use or handle hazardous chemicals that require Safety Data Sheets (SDS)?

Are your company's workers required to wear any specialized PPE for high risk activities?

This does not include any mandatory PPE items such as high visibility clothing, safety boots, safety glasses, or gloves required for visitors.

Examples include, but are not limited to, air purifying respirators, SCBAs, fall protection, welding helmets/goggles, chemical protective clothing, etc.

Do your company's workers operate any heavy mobile equipment or specialized vehicles to carry out your operations?\n\nExamples may include cranes, forklifts, aerial lifts/elevated work platforms, bulldozers, skid steer loaders, front end loaders, excavators, etc.

Is your company required to complete or obtain a “permit to work” in order to conduct any of your operations?\n\nExamples may include confined space permit, hot work permit, energy isolation (LOTO), working at heights, etc.
When visiting sites, are your company's workers escorted 100% of the time?
When visiting sites, will all work or labor be provided within administrative areas?

When providing onsite support services, do you operate equipment, handle chemicals, handle/prepare food, or operate culinary equipment? (i.e. exterminating and pest control, janitorial, landscaping, snow removal, etc.)?

Do services provided by your company occur at self-managed facilities?\n\nSelf-managed facilities are defined as a fixed site/manufacturing or processing facility where your services are conducted independently from your client base.
Are workers potentially exposed to any physical hazards?\n\nExamples may include ergonomic, noise exposure, heat/cold stress, or vibrations.
Are workers potentially exposed to any chemical, biological, or radiological hazards?\n\nExamples may include environmental exposures.
Are workers potentially exposed to hazards due to working with machinery, tools, or equipment?\n\nExamples may include mobile or heavy equipment.
Please select the type of product(s) your company manufactures and/or supplies.

Wood Products, Paper, Printing and Related Support Activities, Plastics and Rubber Products, Nonmetallic Mineral Products (i.e. Clay, Glass, Cement, Concrete, Lime, Gypsum, Abrasive Products), Computer and Electronic Products, Electrical Equipment, Appliance and Component Manufacturing, Furniture and Related Products, and Laboratory and Medical Products and Equipment

Petroleum and Coal Products, Chemicals, Primary Metals (i.e. Iron and Steel Mills, Steel Products from Purchased Steel, Alumina and Aluminum Production and Processing, Nonferrous Metals, Foundries), Fabricated Metal, Machinery, Transportation Equipment
Does your company strictly transport parcels, mail, or packages as a service?
Does your company transport or haul oversized/permit-required loads?
Does your company transport precious cargo?\n\nPrecious cargo is defined as very dear things being moved, typically referring to humans and other living creatures.

At delivery, do drivers come into contact with secured loads, or loads requiring assisted lift?

Examples may include:
-Transferring materials over 50lb (23kg)
-Removal of securing straps, tie down, or chains
-Mobile equipment such as cranes, forklifts, aerial platforms, earth movers, or vehicles
